title: Agency in Language, Alane Suhr | Compile 26
summary: Alane Suhr, Assistant Professor at UC Berkeley, discusses agency in language and what language models fundamentally are. She argues that humans, not the concept of AI itself, hold the agency in how we use and interpret language models.
writeup: Alane Suhr, an assistant professor at UC Berkeley, examines agency in language and what language models fundamentally are in this Compile 26 talk hosted by Cursor. The central argument: agency rests with humans in how language models are used and interpreted, rather than with AI as a concept. The discussion touches on fine-tuning and RLHF as part of framing how these systems are shaped.
